items.title: Feedforward MLP data flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  inp["Input features"] --> h1["Hidden layer 1\nLinear + ReLU"]
  h1 --> h2["Hidden layer 2\nLinear + ReLU"]
  h2 --> outL["Output layer\nLinear + task head"]
  noteH["Hidden layers re-mix\nand bend features"] -.-> h1
  noteH -.-> h2
items.caption: Standard dense MLP: alternating linear maps and nonlinear activations at hidden tiers, then a task-specific output head.

items.title: Linear vs MLP decision boundary (2D intuition)
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  slData["2D points"] --> slLine["One hyperplane boundary"]
  slLabel["Single perceptron"] -.-> slData
  mlpData["Same 2D points e.g. XOR"] --> hiddenFeat["Hidden units carve regions"]
  hiddenFeat --> mlpCurve["Piecewise non-linear boundary"]
  mlpLabel["MLP with hidden layer"] -.-> mlpData
items.caption: A single layer draws one straight separator; hidden layers plus nonlinear activations compose multiple half-space regions into curved or piecewise boundaries.

items.title: Where nonlinearity must appear
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  linOnly["Linear → Linear → Linear"] --> collapse["Collapses to one linear map"]
  withNL["Linear → ReLU → Linear"] --> express["Non-linear input-output map"]
items.caption: Without a nonlinear activation between hidden tiers, depth adds no extra decision-boundary expressivity over a single linear layer.
